description_long: This house is an elevated wooden structure with a thatched gable roof, supported by stilts and featuring a wraparound porch. The main living area is partially enclosed with simple, flat walls and rectangular window openings, while the porch is open with wooden railings. The roof is constructed from overlapping natural materials, likely bamboo or palm, giving it a rustic appearance. Access to the house is provided by a staircase leading up to the porch, which is set above ground level. The house's design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on ventilation and shade. The overall structure suggests a traditional, tropical or rural architectural style suited for warm, humid climates.
description: A stilted wooden house with a thatched gable roof, wraparound porch, and open railings, accessed by an exterior staircase. The design is rustic and functional, with partial wall enclosures and natural materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open porch, partial wall enclosures, and the main entry area with visible window openings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thatched roof, the depth of the porch, and the staircase leading up to the house.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the open back porch, additional window openings, and the extended platform area.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full length of the porch, the stilt supports, and the back entry area.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry via exterior wooden staircase to an open porch with simple railings and no decorative elements.
